title: Prompt Injection, Tool Output Trust, Exfiltration, and Safe Rendering
description: Retrieved text claims to be system instructions, a tool result requests secrets, the agent follows embedded links, approval text is forged, and model Markdown reaches an unsafe renderer. Learners cumulatively produce instruction-data separation and agent security gateway through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent application policy, user instructions, retrieved data, tool output, memory, and provider metadata with explicit authority and non-executable boundaries.
Test direct and indirect goal hijacking, authority spoofing, hidden text, encoded payloads, multimodal instructions, and tool-result injection.
Treat tool names, content, links, files, citations, metadata, error text, and nested model output as untrusted typed data.
